data.title: Options Volatility and Implied Earnings Moves This Week, February 16 – February 19, 2026
data.description:
This week, several major companies will be reporting earnings. Among them are Walmart (WMT), Energy Transfer (ET), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), CoreWeave (CRWV).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ahead of this week's major earnings announcements, TipRanks has calculated the expected moves of these companies' stocks. We use the at-the-money straddle of the options whose post-earnings expiration dates are closest to the earnings announcement date.

Note that options volatility tends to be high in the days leading up to an earnings announcement, due to uncertainty around the earnings. Investors don't know whether the earnings will be positive or negative, so the price of options (aka the option premium) rises.

Furthermore, after the earnings announcement, because the uncertainty has been resolved, the price of options (the option premium) decreases substantially. This is known as the volatility crush.

Here are the major companies reporting this week and their expected earnings moves. Click on any ticker to see additional data about options on the stock, including real-time expected earnings moves, prices, volume, and open interest.

data.title: Alibaba (BABA) Upgrades Qwen AI Model as China's Tech Giants Battle for Users
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) refreshed its flagship AI model with a new Qwen3.5 upgrade, stepping up its push in China's rapidly developing artificial intelligence space.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The updated model can understand text, images, and video and is built to handle "AI agent" tasks, allowing it to act on a user's behalf. It can also review videos up to two hours long, supporting uses such as research, shopping assistance, and automated services.

AI Competition Intensifies in China

The release comes just as China's AI sector prepares for another wave of product launches. Rivals including Baidu (BIDU) and Tencent Holdings (TCEHY), along with several startups, have recently rolled out upgrades of their own. The companies are also preparing for a widely expected new platform from DeepSeek.

The timing also matters because the Lunar New Year holiday brings a surge in app usage across China, making it an important period for testing whether new services can attract and retain users.

Competition is also moving beyond technology. Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent are offering cash rewards and promotions to draw users to their chatbots and secure market share early while adoption is still developing.

Alibaba Expands Its AI Strategy

Alibaba has been increasing its focus on AI since last year's surge in interest in AI models. The company plans to spend tens of billions of dollars on cloud systems and AI tools, seeing the technology as a key growth area beyond online shopping.

The Qwen app is also expanding into a broader service platform linked to Alibaba's ecosystem. Last month, Alibaba upgraded Qwen so users can order food delivery, book travel, and make payments directly within the chat. Users can now handle everyday tasks inside the app through its AI assistants. 

Is Alibaba Stock a Good Buy Right Now?   

Analysts remain highly bullish about Alibaba's stock trajectory. With 15 unanimous Buy ratings, BABA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. Also, the average Alibaba price target of $203.09 implies about 30.41% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: X Prepares to Launch In-App Trading, which Elon Musk Says Is 'Going to Be a Game-Changer'
data.description:
Elon Musk is ready to execute on his plan to turn X into the world's biggest financial platform. X's head of product, Nikita Bier, confirmed that the platform will launch a feature called "Smart Cashtags" in just a few weeks. This new tool will allow users to buy and sell stocks and cryptocurrencies without ever leaving their feed. Musk has high hopes for the new system, recently telling employees during an internal meeting: "This is really intended to be the place where all the money is. It's really going to be a game-changer.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Elon Musk Wants to Facilitate Trading Directly on X

The new system is a massive upgrade from the old, static ticker symbols. Under the new plan, when a user sees a tag like $BTC or $TSLA in a post, they can tap it to see live price charts and execute trades instantly. It's important to note that X isn't trying to become a traditional stockbroker itself; instead, the platform will act as a front door that connects users to existing trading partners and exchanges. The goal is to make investing as easy as liking a post. Musk believes that by removing the friction of switching between different apps, X can capture a huge portion of the hundreds of billions of dollars moved by financial news every day.

The Trading Launch Is Part of X Money

This trading launch is the centerpiece of X Money, the platform's new payments arm. Musk revealed that the system has already finished internal testing with employees and will open to a limited group of external users within the next month or two. Backed by a partnership with Visa (V), X Money will handle everything from peer-to-peer payments, similar to Venmo, to settling global transactions. Musk's vision is to mirror the success of super apps like China's WeChat, where one single application handles your social life, your shopping, and your entire bank account.

Musk's Plan Has Sparked Security Debates

While the plan is ambitious, it has sparked a lot of debate among security experts. Some worry that making it "too easy" to buy assets like Dogecoin could lead to risky, impulsive decisions. To deal with this, X is updating its rules to shut down spammy apps and scam accounts that try to trick people into fake fee pools or raids. Musk's head of product, Nikita Bier, recently said that while they want crypto to "proliferate" on the site, they are strictly blocking any apps that harass users or create unfair incentives for scammers.

Investors can track Elon Musk and his ventures on TipRanks. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: China Ready to Dazzle the World with Humanoid Robots 'Super Bowl' Show
data.description:
Anyone who thought the Super Bowl had been and gone needs to tune into Chinese television this evening where China is ready to show off its humanoid robotics leadership to the world and President Xi.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a Reuters article, humanoid robots will be the star of China's most-watched TV show, the annual CCTV Spring Festival gala. It is comparable in its size and popularity to the Super Bowl in the U.S.

Gala of Innovations

Four humanoid startups – Unitree Robotics, Galbot, Noetix and MagicLab – are set to show off their robotic innovations at the gala. It will take some going to beat last year's event which apparently included 16 full-size Unitree humanoids twirling handkerchiefs and dancing in unison with human – remember them? – performers.

The CCTV show, which drew 79% of live TV viewership in China last year, has for decades been used to highlight Beijing's tech ambitions including its space programme, drones and robotics, ⁠Georg Stieler, Asia managing director and head of robotics and automation at technology consultancy Stieler, told Reuters.

"What distinguishes the gala from comparable events elsewhere is the directness of the pipeline from industrial policy to prime-time spectacle," Stieler said. "Companies that appear on the gala stage receive tangible rewards in government orders, investor attention, and market access."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Ray Dalio Says the Global Order Is Breaking and Investors Could See Bigger Market Shocks
data.description:
Ray Dalio, co-chief investment officer of Bridgewater Associates, has published a long post on X that seeks to put a clean frame around a messy moment, and he makes clear that the shift he sees has a real financial impact on debt, stocks, and cash over time. He cites remarks at the Munich Security Conference, where leaders said the post-1945 world order is no longer in place. He then ties that shift to his own "Big Cycle" view of how global power tends to move over time.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dalio says we are now in what he calls Stage 6, when rules fade, power matters more, and big states push harder for what they want. He writes that this stage brings "great disorder" because "might is right" and there is a "clash of great powers." He also shares a simple rule for how to act in this kind of world: "Have power, respect power, and use power wisely."
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Goldman Sachs Names 3 'Strong Buy' Energy Stocks with Room to Run
data.description:
A mix of factors has lifted oil prices after they hit a 12-month low in December. Heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East – particularly concerns about supply risks linked to Iran – have supported crude benchmarks, as well as ongoing uncertainty around production from sanctioned exporters such as Venezuela. Broader shifts in market sentiment tied to U.S. policy rhetoric and risk appetite have also contributed to volatility in energy markets.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Any sustained rise in oil prices would ripple through the broader economy. However, senior energy analysts at Goldman Sachs argue that volatility can also create opportunity. In a recent note, the team highlighted three energy stocks they believe are well positioned in the current environment.

Goldman's selections span multiple segments of the energy complex, including exploration and production, midstream infrastructure, and utilities, reinforcing the case for diversification.

Each stock also carries a Strong Buy consensus rating on Wall Street, according to TipRanks data. With that context in mind, let's take a closer look at what sets these names apart.

Viper Energy (VNOM)

Goldman's exploration and production (E&P) pick is Viper energy, a limited partnership firm that owns and exploits oil and natural gas assets in North America. The company acquires assets in the form of mineral rights and royalty interests, located in high-margin hydrocarbon areas that are mainly undeveloped. Viper's business model is designed to sustain free cash flow without requiring high demands on capital.

Viper is a subsidiary of the Texas-based E&P firm Diamondback Energy, and uses that relationship to inform its purchases of assets, particularly royalty interests. Diamondback, in turn, is the primary operator on Viper's assets, and has a clear incentive to develop those acres for improved consolidated returns. The mutual relationship benefits both companies – and Viper's shareholders.

The shareholders gain because they are the 'primary business' for Viper. The company has prioritized providing attractive shareholder returns as a business goal, which it does by loading up on mineral and royalty assets in the Permian basin, one of Texas' richest hydrocarbon-bearing formations.

Investors should be aware of a couple of important developments with Viper in recent months. In August of last year, the company acquired Sitio Royalties in an all-equity transaction that brought Sitio's assets under Viper's rubric. The new assets were integrated into Viper's operations during 3Q25.

This was followed in November by the announcement that Viper had entered into an agreement to sell off its non-Permian assets to an affiliate of GRP Energy Capital and Warwick Capital Partners. This transaction was valued at $670 million, and is expected to close during 1Q26.

Viper pays out a 33-cent regular dividend to shareholders, with the last payment sent out on November 20. It was accompanied by a 25-cent variable dividend – Viper has a history in recent years of adding variable dividends to the regular payment. The regular dividend annualizes to $1.32 per common share and gives a 3% forward yield, while the addition of the variable increased the yield to 5.2%.

In 3Q25, the last period reported, Viper realized an adjusted net income of $0.40 per share, beating the forecast by a penny. The company finished Q3 with $53 million in cash and liquid assets on hand.

For Goldman's Neil Mehta, an expert on the energy industry, Viper presents a combination of sound valuation and a positive outlook going forward. He writes of the company, "We see VNOM as a stock with positive valuation support given shares trade at a 10% FCF yield on our 2027 estimates (using $70/bbl Brent oil) – a modest premium relative to oily E&P peer average of 11%. We continue to highlight the benefits of VNOM's no-capex royalty business model which supports VNOM's meaningful return of capital commitments (at least 75% of cash available for distribution to shareholders) and low dividend breakeven of ~$30/bbl WTI. While the announced non-Permian assets sales are still pending close, we remain constructive on the long-term benefits stemming from the close relationship between VNOM and FANG as well as the shareholder return profile of the pro-forma, pure-play Permian business in an uncertain oil environment."

Mehta, a 5-star analyst, puts a Buy rating on VNOM along with a $54 price target that points toward a one-year upside potential of 22%. (To watch Mehta's track record, click here)

The Strong Buy consensus rating on VNOM shares is unanimous, based on 11 recent positive analyst reviews. The stock is trading for $44.25 and its $50.8 average target price implies a gain of 15% in the coming year. (See VNOM stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Strategy Stock Posted that it "Can Withstand a Drawdown in $BTC Price to $8K" as Michael Saylor Doubles Down on His Never Sell Mantra
data.description:
Michael Saylor is standing firm as he addresses fears that his company might be forced to dump its massive Bitcoin (BTC-USD) stash. Saylor recently re-posted a big update from Strategy (MSTR) on X. The company posted that "Strategy can withstand a drawdown in $BTC price to $8K and still have sufficient assets to fully cover our debt." Even at that low level, the company says its assets would still be enough to fully pay off its roughly $6 billion in debt. While the market has been volatile, Saylor is doubling down on his never-sell mantra.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Strategy's Plan Involves Turning Debt into Stock

During a recent appearance on CNBC, Saylor explained that the company is moving toward a strategy called "equitizing" its debt. Instead of worrying about how to pay back billions in cash when loans come due between 2027 and 2032, Strategy plans to gradually convert those loans into company stock. Saylor noted that the company has no interest in getting rid of its digital assets, stating clearly, "We're not going to be selling, we're going to be buying Bitcoin. I expect we'll be buying Bitcoin every quarter forever." He believes this shift away from traditional debt will make the company a secure financial vault that doesn't have to worry about the typical risks of a bank loan.

Why Saylor Isn't Worried about a Crash

Saylor is often asked what happens if the market stays down for years. He told investors that the company has about two and a half years' worth of cash just sitting on its balance sheet to cover interest and dividend payments. "If Bitcoin falls 90% for the next four years, we'll refinance the debt," Saylor said, adding that the company would simply "roll it forward" rather than panic. He views Bitcoin as a long-term asset that will eventually outperform everything else, and he believes lenders will continue to support the company because of that long-term value.

Critics Warn of a Dump on Regular Investors

Despite Saylor's confidence, not everyone thinks this is a clever move. Some analysts argue that turning debt into equity is just a fancy way of diluting the stock. When a company equitizes its debt, it creates new shares, which can make the shares current investors own less valuable. Critics like Anton Golub have gone as far as to call this a planned "dump on retail investors," suggesting that the company is effectively shifting its risk onto everyday people. Skeptics also point out that if Bitcoin really hit $8,000, Strategy would be sitting on a massive paper loss of nearly $48 billion, which could make it very hard to find new lenders willing to help them refinance.

Is Strategy Inc a Buy or Sell?

According to TipRanks, MSTR stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on a unanimous 11 Buy ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month MSTR price target of $359.82 implies 169% upside from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani
